revision.comment: native name already there
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]]

大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia09]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia09]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia25]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Top level categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Top level categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:kk]] [[category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:kk]] [[category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:kk]] [[category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:kk]] [[category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsasahara Shrine
大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia Wikipedia]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]

The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - 大笹原神社 | image = Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|35.43|N|136|3|57.06|E|type:landmark_region:JP_dim:15|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]] | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, [[Yasu, Shiga]], Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle [[Muromachi period]], this building is a 3×3 [[Ken (unit)|''ken'']], single-storied structure with an [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''irimoya-zukuri'']] style {{#tag:ref|(''[[irimoya-zukuri]]'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable [[pediment]]s on the upper part with a [[hip roof|hipped roof]] on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of [[Higashiyama culture]].{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu city]] |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = [[Shiga Prefecture]] |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[category:Ōmi Province]] [[category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Gō-sha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:kk]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:qq]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine}][[category:qq]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:kk]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:mass bump]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:qq]][[en:Ōsasahara Shrine}][[category:qq]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]]] [[category:dd]][[category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:dd]] [[category:dd]][[category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:dd]] [[category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[category:1 revision imported]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:bump 1-26]] [[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[category:Ōsasahara Shrine]][[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]]
