Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, this building is a 3×3 {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=''ken''|1=Ken (unit)}}, single-storied structure with an {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''irimoya-zukuri''|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style {{#tag:ref|(''{{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=East Asian hip-and-gable roof|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable {{ill|Pediment|en|Pediment|WD=Q190996|lt=Pediment|1=Pediment}}s on the upper part with a {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=hipped roof|1=Hip roof}} on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of {{ill|Higashiyama culture|en|Higashiyama culture|WD=Q1036294|lt=Higashiyama culture|1=Higashiyama culture}}.{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu city|1=Yasu, Shiga}} |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:sn_crud|TESTŌsasahara Shrine]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ōsasahara-jinja}} [[ja:大笹原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438451}} [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

大笹原神社 | image = File:Shiga Oosasahara.jpg | caption = The Main Building of Ōsasahara Shrine, a National Treasure | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07649|N|136.06585|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Ōsasahara Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = 986 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōsasahara, {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga|1=Yasu, Shiga}}, Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642|1=Yasu, Shiga}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ōsasahara Shrine'''|大笹原神社| Ōsasahara Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu|1=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine are [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. ==Outline== Ōsasahara Shrine was constructed in the year 986 by Echi Morozane, a local warlord. In 1414, the shrine's main hall (''[[honden]]'') was reconstructed by Mabuchi Sadanobu, the castellan of Iwakura Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. ==Cultural properties== ;Honden: Reconstructed in 1414 during the middle {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, this building is a 3×3 {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=''ken''|1=Ken (unit)}}, single-storied structure with an {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''irimoya-zukuri''|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style {{#tag:ref|(''{{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=East Asian hip-and-gable roof|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}'', 入母屋造): a hip-and-gable roof combining a ridge and two gable {{ill|Pediment|en|Pediment|WD=Q190996|lt=Pediment|1=Pediment}}s on the upper part with a {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=hipped roof|1=Hip roof}} on all sides in the lower part of the roof.{{cite web| url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/i/irimoyazukuri.htm |title = ''irimoya-zukuri''| publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]]| accessdate = 2009-11-08}}|group="ex"|name="irimoya-zukuri-expl"}} roof and a 1 ''ken'' step canopy covered by [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|hinoki cypress]] bark shingles. It is particularly notable for the excellent construction technique of the transom and doors. The ornamental carvings are representative of {{ill|Higashiyama culture|en|Higashiyama culture|WD=Q1036294|lt=Higashiyama culture|1=Higashiyama culture}}.{{cite web |url = http://www.city.yasu.lg.jp/school/shinohara/1464154206656.html |title = 大笹原神社 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine |publisher = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu city|1=Yasu, Shiga}} |accessdate = April 11, 2020 }} {{cite web |url = http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |title = 大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title=Ōsasahara Shrine main hall |publisher = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} |accessdate = November 1, 2009 |url-status = dead |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20110613090900/http://www.pref.shiga.jp/hodo/e-shinbun/ma07/files/20090713_2.pdf |archive-date = June 13, 2011 }} The building was designated as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in 1961. {{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/189283|title=大笹原神社本殿 |trans-title= Ōsasahara Shrine honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;Shinohara Shrine Honden: Located to the left of the main shrine is a subsidiary shrine dedicated to [[Ishikori-dome no Mikoto]]. Built in 1425, it was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1931.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1445|title=大笹原神社境内社篠原神社本殿 |trans-title= Osasahara Shrine Precincts Shinohara Shrine Main Hall |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ===Notes=== {{Reflist|colwidth=|group=ex}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/179/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=495 Shiga Jinja-cho]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:sn_crud|TESTŌsasahara Shrine]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] [[en:Ōsasahara Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ōsasahara-jinja}} [[ja:大笹原神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438451}} [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]]
